2605.22715.title: AnyMo: Geometry-Aware Setup-Agnostic Modeling of Human Motion in the Wild
2605.22715.paper_summary: As wearable and mobile devices become increasingly embedded in daily life, they offer a practical way to continuously sense human motion in the wild. But inertial signals are highly dependent on the sensing setup, including body location, mounting position, sensor orientation, device hardware, and sampling protocol. This setup dependence makes it difficult to learn motion representations that transfer across devices and datasets, and limits the broader use of wearable IMUs beyond closed-set recognition. We introduce AnyMo, a geometry-aware framework for setup-agnostic human motion modeling. AnyMo uses physics-grounded IMU simulation over dense body-surface placements to generate diverse and plausible synthetic signals, pre-trains a graph encoder from paired synthetic placement views and masked partial observations, tokenizes multi-position IMU into full-body motion tokens, and aligns these tokens with an LLM for motion-language understanding. We evaluate AnyMo on three complementary tasks: zero-shot activity recognition across 14 unseen downstream datasets, cross-modal retrieval, and wearable IMU motion captioning, where it improves average Accuracy/F1/R@2 by 11.7\%/11.6\%/22.6\% on HAR, increases zero-shot IMU-to-text and text-to-IMU retrieval MRR by 15.9\% and 28.6\%, respectively, and improves zero-shot captioning BERT-F1 by 18.8\%. These results support AnyMo as a generalist model for wearable motion understanding in the wild. Project page: https://baiyuchen.com/project/AnyMo.
2605.22715.paper_summary_zh: 隨著可穿戴和移動設備越來越深入日常生活，它們提供了一種實用的方法來持續感知人類在自然環境中的運動。<lb/>但是，慣性信號高度依賴於感測設置，包括身體位置、安裝位置、感測器方向、設備硬體和取樣協議。<lb/>這種設置依賴性使得學習能夠跨設備和數據集轉移的運動表示變得困難，並限制了可穿戴IMU在封閉集識別之外的更廣泛應用。<lb/>我們介紹了AnyMo，一個對設置無關的人類運動建模的幾何感知框架。<lb/>AnyMo使用基於物理的IMU模擬，通過密集的身體表面放置來生成多樣且合理的合成信號，從配對的合成放置視圖和遮蔽的部分觀察中預訓練圖編碼器，將多位置IMU標記為全身運動標記，並將這些標記與LLM對齊以實現運動-語言理解。<lb/>我們在三個互補任務上評估AnyMo：在14個未見的下游數據集上進行零樣本活動識別，跨模態檢索，以及可穿戴IMU運動標註，在HAR上平均準確率/F1/R@2提高了11.7\%/11.6\%/22.6\%，零樣本IMU到文本和文本到IMU檢索的MRR分別提高了15.9\%和28.6\%，並且零樣本標註的BERT-F1提高了18.8\%。<lb/>這些結果支持AnyMo作為一個通用模型，用於在自然環境中理解可穿戴運動。<lb/>項目頁面：https://baiyuchen.com/project/AnyMo。
2605.22715.author: Baiyu Chen et.al.
2605.22715.authors: Baiyu Chen, Zechen Li, Wilson Wongso, Lihuan Li, Xiachong Lin, Hao Xue, Benjamin Tag, Flora Salim
